subject: Re: MCore refine_tiltmovies crash
body:
Hi Alister,

Thanks for the update. I will keep an eye on the Github issue and related 
discussions.

Besides this, I have another question and would appreciate any suggestions 
or insights. I'm now working on ribosome, and I realized that even if most 
tomograms contain hundreds of particles, there are always a few tomograms 
with very few particles. I noticed that, you mentioned in some earlier 
discussions that low particle number may lead to overfitting for 
deformation refinement. I'm wondering if this could also cause overfitting 
for tilt movie alignment, or other MCore refinement parameters (e.g. 
defocus). 

If low particle number does affect MCore refinement, I'm considering if 
it's worthwhile to decouple the steps. For example, first I do refinement 
only on those tomograms with sufficient particles to refine parameters 
sensitive to particle number (e.g. deformation), and then do refinement on 
the full dataset to refine the other parameters (e.g. particle pose). In 
this way, no deformation refinement (and other particle-number-sensitive 
parameters) would be done for the tomograms with few particles, but I don't 
know if it's worth the effort since they are just a very small portion of 
the full dataset.

Best,
Shuwen

On Thursday, 3 July 2025 at 19:44:15 UTC+2 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Shuwen,
>
> Looks like you've found a memory leak, thanks for letting us know - I've 
> added an issue on GitHub <https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/396> to 
> track 
>
> Please note there are a few known issues with --refine_tiltmovies e.g. 
> https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/385
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On Thu, 3 Jul 2025 at 08:51, Shuwen Zhang <shuwen...@embl-hamburg.de> 
> wrote:
>
>> Well, I may found another workaround. I changed the angpix_resample in 
>> "MTools create_species" from bin1 pixel size to bin2 pixel size, and now it 
>> works without cash. However, there are still two issues:
>>
>> 1. An error message shows at the beginning, telling me that the tif file 
>> (movie frame) cannot be found in the warp_frameseries folder. It's really 
>> weird because I have never seen this error before.
>>
>> 2. The GPU memory still keeps decreasing from tilt series to tilt series. 
>> This dataset only includes only 65 tilt series. I'm worried that it may 
>> fail if I process a larger dataset.
>>
>> 2025-07-03 14:41:50.578 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10955 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 14:42:25.220 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU

>> #0, 10521 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 14:44:22.053 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10401 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 14:45:08.590 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10274 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 14:45:55.526 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10136 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 14:46:38.348 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10000 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 14:47:18.947 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 9871 MB free:
>> ....
>> 2025-07-03 15:24:51.263 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 3015 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 15:25:26.527 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 2895 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 15:26:17.334 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 2741 MB free:
>> 2025-07-03 15:27:08.382 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 2589 MB free:
>>
>> On Tuesday, 1 July 2025 at 15:34:39 UTC+2 Shuwen Zhang wrote:
>>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> I'm trying to do M refinement on my linux desktop with a single GPU. 
>> While most MCore functions can run without issue, the tilt movie alignment 
>> function fails.
>>
>> The command I'm using is very simple:
>>
>> MCore --population m/250521_st06.population --refine_tiltmovies
>>
>> The program runs very well at the beginning, but stops in the middle and 
>> the output shows the out of memory error:
>>
>> Loading population... Done
>> Creating directories... Done
>> Spawning workers... Done
>> Preparing for refinement – this will take a few minutes per species
>> Preparing refinement requisites...
>> 2/2                                                                       
>>                   
>> Performing refinement
>> Preparing population for data source 250521_st06...Done
>> Loading gain reference for 250521_st06... Done
>> Refining all series in data source...
>> 63/65terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::runtime_error' 
>>                    
>>   what():  cuFFT error: CUFFT_INTERNAL_ERROR at 
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/NativeAcceleration/gtom/src/FFT/FFT.cu:23
>>
>> Unhandled exception. System.NotImplementedException: The method or 
>> operation is not implemented.
>>    at MCore.MCore.WorkerDied(Object sender, EventArgs e) in 
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/MCore/MCore.cs:line 
>> 599
>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.ReportDeath() in 
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 
>> 256
>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper.<StartHeartbeat>b__14_0() in 
>> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1741217580690/work/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 
>> 190
>> Aborted (core dumped)
>>
>> I checked the log files for each tilt series and noticed that the free 
>> GPU memory gradually decreases over time. Here I show the first line of 
>> some of these log files:
>>
>> ==> refine_lam5_ts_010.log <==
>> 2025-07-01 11:30:00.855 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 9742 MB free:
>> ==> refine_lam11_ts_012.log <==
>> 2025-07-01 11:31:06.177 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10756 MB free:
>> ==> refine_lam4_ts_008.log <==
>> 2025-07-01 11:32:01.501 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10632 MB free:
>> ==> refine_lam5_ts_007.log <==
>> 2025-07-01 11:33:08.818 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10564 MB free:
>> ==> refine_lam6_ts_002.log <==
>> 2025-07-01 11:34:16.131 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 10500 MB free:
>> ...
>> ==> refine_lam6_ts_004.log <==
>> 2025-07-01 12:39:28.548 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 6083 MB free:
>> ==> refine_lam6_ts_006.log <==
>> 2025-07-01 12:40:49.023 Received "MPARefine", with 4 arguments, for GPU 
>> #0, 5995 MB free:
>>
>> I was just wondering if the GPU memory is being allocated without release 
>> during processing multiple tilt series. There is actually a workaround 
>> for me by uploading all the data to a cluster to do the refinement, but doing 
>> so would be quite inconvenient. So, is there any solutions to avoid this 
>> problem?
>>
>> Best,
>> Shuwen
>>
>> --

>> .
